name: edit_file_tool
summary: Replace one or more occurrences of a string inside an existing file.
Returns a one-line summary naming how many occurrences were replaced.
constraints: Preserves the rest of the file byte-for-byte. Indentation, trailing newlines, and whitespace must match the file exactly.
The default mode replaces a single occurrence and fails if `old_string` matches more than once. Set `replace_all` to replace every occurrence.
The tool resolves `path` relative to the agent's working directory.
anti_patterns: To create a new file or completely rewrite an existing one — use `write_file_tool`.
To inspect the file before editing — use `read_file_tool` first.
cautions: ALWAYS read the file with `read_file_tool` before editing it; without that, your `old_string` is a guess and is likely to be ambiguous or absent.
IMPORTANT: the tool fails when `old_string` is not unique unless `replace_all` is true. If you see "Found N occurrences", either widen `old_string` with surrounding context or set `replace_all`.
IMPORTANT: the tool fails when `old_string` is not found at all. Re-read the file to confirm the text you expect is still there.
IMPORTANT: the file's previous content is overwritten in place; recovery requires version control or a backup made before the call.
output: Plain text. On success: `Edited <path>: replaced <count> occurrence(s)`. On failure: an error message naming the cause (file unreadable, `old_string` absent, multiple matches without `replace_all`, or write failure).

path.description: Path to the file to edit, relative to the working directory.

old_string.description: Exact text to find. Must appear verbatim in the file, including indentation and trailing whitespace.

new_string.description: Replacement text. May be empty to delete the match.

replace_all.description: Replace every occurrence (default: false). When false, the tool fails if `old_string` matches more than once.
